ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn class for Prawn Caesar Salad; mapped to the nearest parent class 'salad food product' (FOODON_00002473). A more specific class for seafood Caesar salad does not exist in the current FoodOn release.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers composite salad dishes; no specific code for prawn Caesar salad exists. This is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps directly to Prawn Caesar Salad as a food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Prawn Caesar Salad is approximately 250 g (one plate), comprising romaine lettuce, dressed prawns, Caesar dressing, Parmesan shavings, and croutons.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn Caesar Salad — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Prawn Caesar Salad (cr_id G00010649). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed green salad with dressing (GI ~15)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Prawn Caesar Salad. The GI is estimated at approximately 15 (low) based on the closest analogous food — mixed green salad with dressing — from the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is very low at 3.3 g/100g, predominantly from croutons and dressing sugars. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (15 × 3.3 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 1.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1210 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; potassium is negligible at 29 mg/100g, providing no counterbalancing effect
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Prawn Caesar Salad due to its very high sodium content (1210 mg/100g); if consumed, request dressing on the side, omit Parmesan and croutons, and limit portion to no more than 100 g to keep sodium intake within safe bounds.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate is very low at 3.3 g/100g with sugar at 2.81 g/100g, posing minimal glycaemic impact; estimated GI ~15 (low)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Prawn Caesar Salad is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes given its very low carbohydrate content and low estimated glycaemic index; however, croutons should be omitted or minimised to avoid refined carbohydrate spikes, and Caesar dressing should be verified for added sugars.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat is very high at 57.8 g/100g with estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g; sodium at 1210 mg/100g further elevates cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should exercise caution with Prawn Caesar Salad due to its very high total fat (57.8 g/100g), elevated saturated fat (~9.5 g/100g), and very high sodium (1210 mg/100g); substituting Caesar dressing with olive oil and lemon and omitting croutons and Parmesan would substantially reduce cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; processed Caesar dressing may contain pro-inflammatory trans fats
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute Caesar dressing with olive oil and lemon juice to reduce saturated and trans fat intake, as the dressing's fat profile is the primary pro-inflammatory concern in this dish.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Prawn Caesar Salad is a Western-origin composite salad dish served in Singapore and adapted to local dining contexts. It consists of fresh or cooked prawns (shrimp) arranged over crisp romaine lettuce leaves, dressed with classic Caesar dressing — an emulsified sauce made from egg yolk, anchovies, garlic, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, and Parmesan cheese — and typically garnished with croutons and additional Parmesan shavings. The dish delivers a rich, creamy, and savoury flavour profile with textural contrast between the tender prawns, crunchy croutons, and crisp lettuce. Nutritionally, the dish is calorie-dense at 542 kcal/100g, driven almost entirely by its very high fat content (57.8 g/100g), which reflects the oil-heavy Caesar dressing. Protein is low at 2.17 g/100g relative to the caloric density, and carbohydrates are minimal at 3.3 g/100g, making it a low-glycaemic option. However, sodium is extremely high at 1210 mg/100g, making it unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease without significant modification. The dish is not vegetarian, not vegan, and contains major allergens including crustaceans, fish (anchovies), eggs, milk (Parmesan), and gluten (croutons). It is commonly served as a starter or light main course at lunch or dinner in Singapore restaurants and cafes.

Is shrimp caesar salad keto friendly?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Prawn Caesar Salad (cr_id G00010649) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and provenance is canonical. Saturated fat is estimated from the fat profile of Caesar dressing components as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; this single field is medium confidence.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions (monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans), full vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and moisture data for this composite dish are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010649 as of 2026-04-16; the HPB Foodo database was the approved source consulted and confirmed these fields are not available for this record. saturated_fat_g estimated at 9.5 g/100g based on the typical fatty acid profile of Caesar dressing (predominantly soybean or canola oil with egg yolk and Parmesan); this is an approximation as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this entry. GI estimated at ~15 (low) using the closest analogous food (mixed green salad with dressing) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Prawn Caesar Salad exists in the approved GI source. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (15 × 3.3 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 1.2. Cultural classification corrected: the input record incorrectly listed primary_cuisine as 'Indian'; Prawn Caesar Salad is a Western/International dish adapted for the Singapore market. Halal status is not confirmed as Caesar dressing typically contains anchovies and Worcestershire sauce (which may contain non-halal ingredients); is_halal set to false pending verification. All core macros (protein_g 2.17, fat_g 57.8, carbohydrate_g 3.3) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification is required. Aliases self-check: all strings in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in flat aliases array — 'Prawn Caesar Salad', 'Caesar Salad with Prawns', 'Shrimp Caesar Salad', 'Caesar Prawn Salad', '虾凯撒沙拉', '蝦凱撒沙拉', 'Salad Caesar Udang', 'இறால் சீசர் சாலட்', 'झींगा सीज़र सलाद' all confirmed present.
